LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JAN-26-2023 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PROGRESSIVE INSURANCE CO.'S UNOPPOSED "DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT" IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH STATES THAT THE MOTION WAS NOT OPPOSED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. LAW AND MOTION DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE INSURANCE COMPANY'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO PROSCECUTE IS GRANTED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A MODIFIED PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM TO THE COURT'S RULING AND IS TO INCLUDE LANGUGE REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO TIMELY FILE AN OPPOSITION AND TO TIMELY NOTIFY THE COURT AND COUNSEL OF HIS INTENT TO CONTEST THE COURT'S TETNATIVE RULING. HEARING RE: DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court notes that the plaintiff did not properly contest the Court's tentative ruling nor did plaintiff file an opposition.

The Court hears argument from the plaintiff.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Progressive Insurance Co.'s unopposed "demurrer to plaintiffs' first amended complaint" is sustained with leave to amend.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-04-2023 Continued To Mar-15-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Dec-15-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances
Noah Zizer, 510-258-3801, Plaintiff in Pro Per. (In-Person)
Cameron A. Jones, Esq., BREMER WHYTE BROWN & O'MEARA LLP, (510) 540-4881, cjones@bremerwhyte.com, for Defendant PROGRESSIVE INSURANCE COMPANY, Via ZOOM
Mia Ahn, Esq., DANIELS, FINE, ISRAEL, SCHONBUCH & LEBOVITS, LLP, (310) 556-7900, for Defendants KEMPER CORPORATION and ALLIANCE UNITED INSURANCE COMPANY Via ZOOM

(Mai Ahn, counsel for defendants KEMPER CORPORATION and ALLIANCE UNITED INSURANCE COMPANY present on ZOOM before the start of the 9:30 a.m. L & M Calendar. Notice of Contest was not received by the Court or by all other parties. Ms. Ahn leaves the ZOOM Room. Plaintiff appears in person to contest the matter without proper notice of contest to the Court and parties. Cameron A. Jones, Esq., counsel for Defendant PROGRESSIVE INSURANCE COMPANY, Via ZOOM but does not contest the Court's tentative ruling. Court states that he will move forward with the hearing, but that most likley tentative ruling will be adopted.)

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

FENDANTS ALLIANCE UNITED INSURANCE COMPANY and KEMPER CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication Of Issues.

Defendants' (ALLIANCE UNITED INSURANCE COMPANY, KEMPER CORPORATION) motion for summary judgment is granted.

The undisputed evidence demonstrates that defendants are mere insurers and it is improper to name then as defendants in an action against their insured, who is the alleged tortfeasor. (See Coleman v. Republic Indemnity Ins. Co. (2005) 132 Cal.App.4th 403 [third party claimants generally cannot sue insurers]; Shaolian v. Safeco Ins. Co. (1999) 71 Cal.App.4th 268, 271["Because the insurer's duties flow to its insured alone, a third party claimant may not bring a direct action against an insurance company."]; Undisputed Material Facts, 1, 4.) Plaintiff fails to show that an exception to this rule applies in this case (see Harper v. Wausau Ins. Co. (1997) 56 Cal.App.4th 1079, 1086) and defendants further establish that there is no basis to plead a claim against them. The motion is therefore granted.

In addition, plaintiff failed to provide a responsive separate statement in compliance with CCP 437c(b)(3) and that alone is grounds to grant the motion.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
